daily_brief: Military tensions dominated today's news as the US and Iran exchanged strikes following attacks on three oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, threatening the fragile ceasefire between the two nations [BBC, Globe and Mail, Al Jazeera]. The escalation overshadowed the NATO summit in Ankara, where Secretary-General Mark Rutte backed the US response while leaders unveiled major defense initiatives, including a £37 billion European missile project and a Canada-led defense bank with nine member nations [BBC, France 24, Globe and Mail]. President Trump expressed disappointment with NATO allies and renewed demands for control of Greenland, which Danish Prime Minister Frederiksen firmly rejected [RFI, France 24]. In France, Marine Le Pen announced her 2027 presidential bid hours after an appeals court upheld her embezzlement conviction but allowed her to run while wearing an electronic tag [BBC Europe]. Russia continues to face domestic fuel shortages severe enough to affect Moscow, even as Ukraine struck Russian tankers ahead of the NATO summit [BBC]. Meanwhile, Typhoon Maysak caused widespread devastation in southern China, killing at least 21 people and displacing tens of thousands [BBC].

top_stories.headline: US and Iran Exchange Strikes After Tanker Attacks in Strait of Hormuz, Threatening Ceasefire
bullets: Three oil tankers were struck in the Strait of Hormuz, prompting US Central Command to launch strikes on more than 80 targets in southern Iran including Qeshm Island, Bandar Abbas, and Sirik
Iran's Revolutionary Guards retaliated by firing missiles and drones at US military facilities in Bahrain and Kuwait, claiming to have hit 85 targets
The US revoked a temporary sanctions waiver that had allowed Iran to sell oil on international markets, and oil prices rose more than 3%
Both sides accused each other of violating the memorandum of understanding signed last month, with negotiations expected to resume after the funeral of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Detailed factual account with NATO chief Rutte's supportive comments prominently featured

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Emphasized impact on ceasefire agreement and oil markets

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Focused on Iran's economic challenges and longer-term recovery prospects

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Led with oil price impact and supply fears

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Provided context on Khamenei funeral and diplomatic timeline

top_stories.headline: NATO Summit in Ankara Reveals Defense Spending Surge and Transatlantic Tensions
bullets: Twelve countries including the UK announced a £37 billion ($50 billion) 'Deep Precision Strike' missile project to protect Europe, capable of striking targets up to 1,250 miles away
Nine countries including Canada, Ukraine, and Turkey formally committed to establishing a new multinational Defense, Security and Resilience Bank to begin operations by 2027

President Trump expressed disappointment with NATO, criticized UK, France, Germany and Italy for insufficient support in the Iran conflict, and suggested he might have boycotted the summit if not for his relationship with Turkey's Erdogan
NATO Secretary-General Rutte stated the US remains 'completely committed' to the alliance and that allies increased spending to around 4% of GDP last year

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized UK's leading role in missile project and Starmer's position

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Highlighted Rutte's reassurances about US commitment

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Focused on Canada's defense bank initiative and South Korea's submarine contract loss

sources.name: The Economist
sources.framing_note: Analysis pieces on NATO's strategic challenges and flattery of Trump

top_stories.headline: Marine Le Pen Announces 2027 Presidential Run Despite Embezzlement Conviction
bullets: A Paris appeals court upheld Le Pen's conviction for misusing €2.8 million in EU funds in a fake jobs scheme but reduced her sentence to wearing an electronic tag for one year
Le Pen announced her presidential candidacy within hours of the ruling, launching a social media campaign with the slogan 'Pour la France - La Renaissance'
She declared she will appeal to France's highest civil court, the Court of Cassation, which suspends the tag requirement until a verdict expected early 2027
Public prosecutors also announced they will appeal the reduced sentence

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Focused on the political gamble and her defiant tone

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Provided detailed background on her three previous presidential runs and rivalry dynamics with Jordan Bardella

top_stories.headline: Typhoon Maysak Kills at Least 21 in China, Triggers Floods and Rare Tornadoes
bullets: Typhoon Maysak caused severe flooding in Guangxi province, particularly in Nanning and surrounding villages, with water completely submerging first floors of homes
At least 17 people died in tornadoes triggered by the typhoon in Hubei province, hundreds of kilometers from the storm's center, with hundreds more injured
At least four additional deaths occurred in Guangxi, with tens of thousands evacuated and dam walls breaking
President Xi Jinping ordered 'all out' rescue and relief operations in response to the disaster

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Detailed human interest accounts from affected villagers, noted typhoons are common at this time of year

top_stories.headline: Russia Faces Severe Fuel Shortages as Ukraine Strikes Continue
bullets: Moscow residents are experiencing widespread fuel shortages with long queues at most petrol stations and many stations closed after running out entirely
Ukrainian forces struck several Russian tankers overnight ahead of the NATO summit, killing at least seven people in broader strikes
Residents reported prices rising and hours-long waits to fill up, with some uncertain about planned road trips
The fuel crisis affects even Russia's wealthy capital despite the country's vast oil resources

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: On-the-ground reporting from Moscow with resident interviews

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Mentioned Ukrainian tanker strikes in context of NATO summit

additional_stories.headline: Denmark's PM Frederiksen tells Trump that Greenland is 'not for sale' as US president renews demands at NATO summit
Hungary halts public TV news broadcasts and suspends state radio in bid to dismantle Orbán-era propaganda apparatus
IOC provisionally lifts Russia's suspension, paving way for full team at 2028 Los Angeles Olympics despite Ukraine objections
Twin bomb attacks wound 18 near Damascus hotel where Macron stayed during landmark Syria visit
US authorities name 37 wanted individuals including gangster Lawrence Bishnoi and a Punjab police officer for transnational crimes
Argentina complete dramatic World Cup comeback, beating Egypt 3-2 after trailing 2-0 with 15 minutes remaining
China defends South Pacific ballistic missile test as 'routine' after New Zealand and regional nations criticize launch
IMF sharply downgrades Cambodia's economic outlook, projecting 3% growth and doubled inflation amid border conflict and energy costs
Indian UN Mission building in Manhattan evacuated after nearby under-construction high-rise becomes structurally unstable
Two Israeli NGOs warn of 'de facto annexation at unprecedented pace' in the West Bank
